titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Louis Chevalier Papers
titlestmt.author: Preliminary Inventory by Diane E. Kaplan

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 08:37

did.unittitle: Louis Chevalier Papers
did.abstract: Research notes, reference files, census documents, student papers on Paris, clippings, and photographs related to Chevalier's work on the history and demography of Paris. Included also are the manuscripts of two of his books: L'Assassinat de Paris and Montmartre du Plaisir et du Crime . The files also contain reference material used by Chevalier in his courses at the College de France. There is also a small amount of professional correspondence with Chevalier's American friends and colleagues.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Louis Chevalier Papers (MS 1122). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The original census sheets for selected streets in Paris, 1946 are available on microfilm (4,131 frames on 4 reels, 35mm.) from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, at cost. Order no. HM151.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: University professor and political scientist; born in Vendeé, France; educated at Ecole Normale Supérieure; professor College de France; professor Institut d'Etudes Politiques de Paris; former member Conseil supérieur de la Recherche Scientifique; former President du Conseil Scientifique du Centre Int. d'Etude des Problémes Humains de Monaco; former member Conseil Economique et Social du District de Paris; author of many books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research notes, reference files, census documents, student papers on Paris, clippings, and photographs related to Chevalier's work on the history and demography of Paris. Included also are the manuscripts of two of his books: L'Assassinat de Paris and Montmartre du Plaisir et du Crime . The files also contain reference material used by Chevalier in his courses at the College de France. There is also a small amount of professional correspondence with Chevalier's American friends and colleagues.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Inventory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Louis Chevalier Papers, the gift to Manuscripts and Archives of Professor Chevalier in 1982, consist of course material, research notes, reference files, student papers, clippings, photographs, manuscripts and correspondence relative to Chevalier's work on the history and demography of Paris. The files contain reference material for Chevalier's courses at the College de France as well as his books.

The papers are roughly arranged in five subject categories established by Chevalier. The first category contains much research material on the ethnic make-up of Paris and on the history of immigration to the city. Files on immigrants from Brittany are particularly voluminous. A second category consists of files on the various sections and neighborhoods of Paris. Of particular importance in this category are two feet of 1946 household census forms for selected streets in Paris. Though the summary of the census has been published the rest of the raw data probably has been destroyed. (Because of the fragile nature of the forms this material has been microfilmed. Researchers will not be allowed to use the originals without first consulting the film.) The third and fourth categories consist of reference files organized by subject for two of Chevalier's recent books, L'Assassinat de Paris and Montmartre du Plaisir et du Crime respectively. The final category includes correspondence with Chevalier's American friends and colleagues.

The material remains in very much the order Chevalier sent it with his notes of identification in place. No hardware has been removed and no preservation measures (except the microfilm) have been undertaken.

did.unittitle: I
La caractère ethnique sous l'Ancien Regime: reference material notes, course material, student papers
La caractère ethnique, premier moitié du XIXe siecle: research notes, drafts
Immigration à Paris, 1850-1914: drafts, notes, reference material
Immigration à Paris à l'époque contemporaire (divers): reference material, printed matter, clippings, notes
Les Parisiens, caractère ethnique: notebook
Les Ethniques: reference files, course material
Les Espagnols à Paris: reference material, survey forms course material
Les Portugais: reference material
Les Bretons de Paris: reference material, student papers correspondence, clippings, notes
Les Bretons de Paris: reference material, correspondence, survey forms
Les Italiens à Paris: reference material, student paper
Les Nord-Africans à Paris: reference material
Caractère ethnique, 1960: reference material, notes
II
La ville et ses quartiers: course notes
Les quartiers: course notes
Le problème du quartiers: general data
Les quartiers(divers): reference material, course material, student papers
Les quartiers(divers): reference material, notes, student papers
Le Auvergnate de Paris: reference material, manuscript
Les Halles: reference material, clippings, maps
Les Halles: reference material, clippings, notes, correspondence
Divers "Halles" "Belleville": correspondence, books, printed material, student papers
Belleville: notes, reference material, clippings, student papers
Belleville: student paper
La Banlieue Nord: reference material, student papers, clippings, photographs, questionnaires
La Banlieue Nord: reference material
La Banlieue, leçons et documents: clippings, notes, background material
La Banlieue (District) [District de la région de Paris], 1968-1974 reference material, clippings
La Banlieue (District), 1968-74: clippings
1958-1960: miscellaneous reference material dating earlier and going later than the dates specified by Chevalier; includes some material used in Les Parisiens .

Les Feuilles de Ménage du recensement de 1946: organized by street - la rue de Gravillers, la rue aux Ours, la rue Reunion, la rue St. Martin, la rue Bourg l'Abbe, Impasse Berthaud, la rue du Maure, la rue Quincampoix

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 151 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Les Feuilles de Ménage du recensement de 1946: organized by street - la rue Quincampoix, la rue Michel le Conte, la rue de Granier St. Lazare, la rue Passage St. Avoyo, la rue Brantene, la rue de Vertus, la rue St. Martin, la rue Chapon, la rue Beaubourg

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material has been microfilmed. Patrons must use HM 151 instead of the originals.

L'Assassinat de Paris : book manuscript, notes, reference material

Dossier 8 (no. 7)
IV
Montmartre du Plaisir et du Crime : book manuscript, notes, reference material
Dossier 1, Les premiers essais sur le livre, Introduction
Dossier 2, La premiere moitié du XIX siecle
Dossier 3, Le second empire et le commun
Dossier 3, Le second empire et le commun
Dossier 4, L'evenement
Dossier 5
Dossier 6
Dossier 6
Dossier 7
Dossier 8
Dossier 8
V
Montmartre, quartier place clichy: photographs
Correspondence du colleagues et amis americains: a selection
Miscellaneous material found in the books sent by Chevalier (these books were given to Acquisitions)
